what_it_does: Renders the right-hand-man chat region (RHM) for the canvas app: a header with minimize control, conversation thread selector, memory record/debrief controls, a graded chat log, a click-to-indicate locus chip, a proposal/approval affordance, and a voice/text input bar. A studio prop hides developer-oriented affordances for design-review mode.
observations: Heavy use of useApp for chat, voice, thread, and indication state/actions.
Local minimize state toggled in the header.
Conversation threads UI has + new and a select to reopen past conversations.
Memory loop controls (record/debrief) are rendered only when studio is false.
Chat messages carry gold (user) and working (assistant) grade badges via inline spans.
Indicated locus chip has no data-ui-ref on the chip or its clear button, per the comment.
ProposeAffordance is rendered unconditionally as the consent gate for proposed actions.
Mic button title and icon switch based on cfg.voice_input_mode (auto_listen vs push_to_talk).
Comments document the retirement of the .rhm-cfg config panel and migration of model/persona/voice settings to A3 consolidated Settings.
threads.map uses an explicit any type.
standouts: Intentional retirement of an internal settings gear panel in favor of centralized A3 Settings.
Gold vs working grade markers on every message.
studio prop that selectively hides dev affordances for non-developer reviewers.
Click-to-indicate and ProposeAffordance as consent/locus interaction patterns.
conventions: Functional React component using hooks and AppContext.
Local UI state (minimize) kept inside the component.
Architecture decisions embedded in source comments.
Conditional rendering driven by flags (studio, indicated, recordingSession).
concerns: cfg.voice_input_mode || 'push_to_talk' is repeated inline in the mic button.
Large destructuring from useApp makes the component's dependencies hard to trace.
Long conditional title string inline in the mic button.
No visible handling of errors or loading states for thread actions.
notes: RhmChat is the chat-local surface: it keeps threads, memory loop, minimize, and input here while model/persona/voice settings have been moved to the centralized A3 Settings.
questions: What is the full contract and return shape of useApp?
What is the exact TypeScript interface for chat message objects (t.role, t.text)?
How does ProposeAffordance receive proposal data — props, context, or global state?
What does indicate(null) do and how is the indicated address formatted?
Which parent components pass studio=true and how is that decision made?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the implementation: it renders the RHM chat region and has retired its duplicated config panel, keeping only chat-local concerns.
intention_signals: The component is trying to be a focused chat surface that avoids duplicated settings UI, surfaces memory/debrief workflows, and supports both developer canvas use and non-developer design review via a studio prop.
novelty: Explicit in-source documentation of retiring an internal config panel, plus gold/working grade badges on every message and a studio mode that hides dev affordances.
contribution: Provides the chat-region UI organ with thread management, memory session controls, message grading, locus indication, and consent-gated proposal rendering; its contribution is the chat-local interaction surface and the documented decision to keep settings centralized elsewhere.
summary_for_embedding: RhmChat.tsx exports a React component that renders the right-hand-man chat panel, including header/minimize, conversation threads, memory record/debrief, graded chat log, indicated-locus chip, proposal affordance, and voice/text input.
intention_for_embedding: This file intends to implement the chat-local region while retiring duplicated configuration UI in favor of centralized settings, supporting both canvas and studio review modes.

symbols.does: Exports the RhmChat React component that renders the right-hand-man chat region.
symbols.description: RhmChat is a functional React component consuming AppContext to render the chat panel. It manages a local minimize state, renders chat-local controls for threads and memory sessions, displays a graded message log, shows an indicated locus chip, renders the ProposeAffordance consent gate, and provides a voice/text input bar. A studio prop hides developer-specific affordances for design-review scenarios.
